mnemonics.title: Five Components of Data Communication
mnemonics.content: SENDER-MESSAGE-MEDIUM-PROTOCOL-RECEIVER (Use: 'Smart Mice Make Perfect Rodent Eats')

mnemonics.title: Data Flow Modes
mnemonics.content: S-H-F: Simplex → Half → Full (increasing capability). Remember: 'S-H-F' = Single-Half-Full duplex, think of progression in traffic flow.

mnemonics.title: Nyquist Formula
mnemonics.content: 2B × log₂M. Remember: 'Nylan is Too Busy Laughing' → Nyquist = 2 × Bandwidth × log₂(M)

mnemonics.title: Shannon Formula
mnemonics.content: C = B × log₂(1+S/N). Remember: 'Cats Have Big Little Sneakers' → Capacity = Bandwidth × log(1 + Signal/Noise)

mnemonics.title: Transmission Impairments
mnemonics.content: A-N-D: Attenuation, Noise, Delay. Remember: 'And' connects them all as problems to overcome.

acronyms.usage: Common copper cable for Ethernet networks, cost-effective for LANs
Twisted pair with shielding for better EMI resistance
Line coding scheme where signal doesn't return to zero between bits
Combining multiple signals using different frequency bands
Combining signals by allocating time slots to each channel
Reference model for network communication standards

visualTips.visualization: Think of a flashlight beam becoming dimmer as it travels farther - signal weakens like light fading in foggy distance, measured in decibels showing gradual decrease.
Bandwidth is the width of a highway (number of lanes), throughput is actual cars passing per minute - highway may have 4 lanes but only 1 lane moving due to traffic.
Picture a wheel with spokes - hub is center, computers are at spoke ends; if center breaks, all spokes disconnect.
Think of sound waves - clap hands and hear it later; light from stars traveled millions of years to reach us - the travel time is propagation delay.

analogies.analogy: Protocol is like a language and etiquette for diplomats - without agreed rules, communication fails.
Encoding is like Morse code - converting letters (data) into dots and dashes (signals) for transmission.
Noise in communication is like background chatter at a crowded party - makes it hard to hear the actual message.
Bandwidth is like pipe diameter - wider pipe (more bandwidth) lets more water (data) flow through at once.
Network topology is like city road layouts - some cities have grid patterns (mesh), others have ring roads (ring), or one main street (bus).
